revision.comment: 出典追加、追記、テンプレート：複数の問題を除去。
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 月田古墳群 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=前橋市}} |画像キャプション = |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[粕川町月田]]・[[粕川町室沢]] |位置 = |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 26|緯度秒 = 11 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 12|経度秒 = 38 |ISO = |形状 = |規模 = |埋葬施設 = |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = 6世紀末～7世紀後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gunma |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }} '''月田古墳群'''（つきだこふんぐん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[粕川町月田]]・[[粕川町室沢]]にある[[古墳時代]]後期の[[古墳群]]。
== 概要 == 月田古墳群は、群馬県前橋市粕川町月田・粕川町室沢に位置する35～40基の古墳からなるが、その多くが既に消滅している{{Sfn|小島|1982|loc=序}}{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=35}}。
昭和23年から昭和30年にかけて[[尾崎喜左雄]]ら[[群馬大学]]考古学研究室によって6基の古墳の発掘調査が行われた{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=16}}。このとき調査された古墳については6世紀末から7世紀後葉の古墳と結論づけられた{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=48}}が、昭和55・56年度の[[粕川村 (群馬県)|粕川村]]教育委員会による7基の古墳に関する調査ではむしろ7世紀後半の古墳が主体であると指摘されている{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=16}}。
自治体から史跡指定を受けている古墳は以下の2基。
* 鏡手塚古墳 - 群馬県指定史跡（昭和24年2月8日指定） * 壇塚古墳 - 群馬県指定史跡（昭和26年10月5日指定）<ref>{{Cite web |title=県指定文化財一覧{{!}}前橋市 |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-04-21 |language=ja}}</ref>
==主要古墳一覧==
{| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small;width:60%" |+ 主要古墳の詳細 ! 古墳名<ref name=":0">群馬県古墳綜覧 本文一覧表編 群馬県教育委員会 P93 707番から714番（2017）より</ref> !綜覧古墳番号<ref>上毛古墳綜覧 群馬県史蹟名勝天然紀念物調査報告書第5輯 群馬縣（1938）</ref> ! 規模<ref name=":0" /> ! 形状<ref name=":0" /> !主体部 !所在地<ref name=":0" /> !調査 ! 備考 |- ! !粕川村11 | | | |粕川村室沢 |昭和55年度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} | |- ! !粕川村12 | | |両袖型横穴式石室{{Sfn|小島|1982|pp=5-11}} |粕川町室沢 |昭和55年度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} |7世紀後半{{Sfn|小島|1982|pp=5-11}} |- ! !粕川村16 | | | |粕川町月田 |昭和55年度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} | |- ! !粕川村26 |14 m{{Sfn|小島|1982|pp=12-16}} |円墳{{Sfn|小島|1982|pp=12-16}} |両袖型横穴式石室{{Sfn|小島|1982|pp=12-16}} |粕川町月田 |昭和56年度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} |7世紀後半{{Sfn|小島|1982|pp=12-16}} |- ! !粕川村34 | | | |粕川町月田 |昭和56年度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} | |- ! 月田薬師塚古墳 !粕川村35{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=43-44}} | 不明{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=43-44}} | 円墳{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=43-44}} |横穴式石室{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=43-44}} |粕川町月田 188 |有{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=43-44}} | |- ! 壇塚古墳 !粕川村36 | 25 [[メートル|m]]{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=35-39}} | 円墳{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=35-39}} |横穴式石室{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=35-39}} |粕川町月田 207 |昭和25年{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=35-39}} | 県史跡<ref>群馬県史跡 昭和26年10月5日指定 第5号</ref> |- ! 鏡手塚古墳 !粕川村44{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=39-42}} | 28 m{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=39-42}} | 前方後円墳{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=39-42}} |片袖型横穴式石室{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=39-42}} |粕川町月田 213外 |昭和23年{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=39-42}} | 県史跡<ref>群馬県史跡 昭和24年2月8日指定 第2号</ref> |- ! 西塚古墳 !粕川村45{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=42-43}} | 20 m{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=42-43}} | 前方後円墳{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=42-43}} |両袖型横穴式石室{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=42-43}} |粕川町月田 213 |昭和23年{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=42-43}} | |- ! 月田丸塚古墳（丸山古墳） !粕川村42 | 不明 | 円墳 | |粕川町月田 218-4 | | 消滅 |- ! 長峯古墳 !粕川村43{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=44-46}} | 11 m{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=44-46}} | 円墳 |片袖型横穴式石室{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=44-46}} |粕川町月田 257-1外 |昭和25年{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=44-46}} | 消滅 |- !丸塚古墳 !粕川村46{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=46}} | |円墳{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=46}} |横穴式石室{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=46}} |粕川町月田 |昭和25年{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=46}} | |- ! 地蔵塚古墳 !粕川村48 | 不明 | 帆立貝型古墳 | |粕川町月田 1919-1外 | | |- ! 月田二子塚古墳 !粕川村49 | 不明 | 前方後円墳 | |粕川町月田 1918-1外 | | 消滅 |- ! !（記載漏1号） | | | | |昭和55年度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} | |- ! !（記載漏2号） | | | | |昭和56年度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} | |}

== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 ==
* {{Cite book|和書 |first= |last=粕川村誌編纂委員会 |title=粕川村誌 |origdate=1972-12-10 |date=1972-12-10 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/101858 |ncid=BN0348760X |doi=10.24484/sitereports.101858}} * {{Cite book|和書 |first=純一 |last=小島 |title=月田古墳群B1 |origdate=1982-03 |date=1982-03 |url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15291 |ncid=BN02071628 |doi=10.24484/sitereports.15291 |series=粕川村文化財報告 |volume=3}} {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=群馬県}} {{デフォルトソート:つきたこふんくん}} [[Category:前橋市の歴史]] [[Category:古墳群]] [[Category:群馬県指定史跡]] [[Category:群馬県の古墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 月田古墳群 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=前橋市}} |画像キャプション = |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[粕川町月田]]・[[粕川町室沢]] |位置 = |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 26|緯度秒 = 11 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 12|経度秒 = 38 |ISO = |形状 = |規模 = |埋葬施設 = |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = 6世紀末～7世紀後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gunma |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }} '''月田古墳群'''（つきだこふんぐん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[粕川町月田]]・[[粕川町室沢]]にある[[古墳時代]]後期の[[古墳群]]。
== 概要 == 月田古墳群は、群馬県前橋市粕川町月田・粕川町室沢に位置する35～40基の古墳からなるが、その多くが既に消滅している{{Sfn|小島|1982|loc=序}}{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=35}}。
1948年（昭和23年）から1955年（昭和30年）にかけて[[尾崎喜左雄]]ら[[群馬大学]]考古学研究室によって6基の古墳の[[発掘調査]]が行われた{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=16}}。このとき調査された古墳については6世紀末から7世紀後葉の古墳と結論づけられた{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=48}}が、1980年（昭和55年）・1981年（昭和56年）度の[[粕川村 (群馬県)|粕川村]]教育委員会による7基の古墳に関する調査ではむしろ7世紀後半の古墳が主体であると指摘されている{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=16}}。
自治体から史跡指定を受けている古墳は以下の2基。
* 鏡手塚古墳 - 群馬県指定史跡（1949年（昭和24年）2月8日指定） * 壇塚古墳 - 群馬県指定史跡（1951年（昭和26年）10月5日指定）<ref>{{Cite web |title=県指定文化財一覧{{!}}前橋市 |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-04-21 |language=ja}}</ref>

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Tsukida Kofun Cluster''' (月田古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient burial mounds) from the late [[Kofun period]] located in {{ill|Maebashi|ja|前橋市}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|ja|群馬県}}, Japan, specifically in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢}}. == Overview == The Tsukida Kofun Cluster comprises 35–40 burial mounds located in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田}} and {{il..."

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 = 月田古墳群 |画像 = {{画像募集中|cat=前橋市}} |画像キャプション = |別名 = |所属 = |所在地 = [[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[粕川町月田]]・[[粕川町室沢]] |位置 = |緯度度 = 36|緯度分 = 26|緯度秒 = 11 |経度度 = 139|経度分 = 12|経度秒 = 38 |ISO = |形状 = |規模 = |埋葬施設 = |出土品 = |陪塚 = |築造時期 = 6世紀末～7世紀後半 |被葬者 = |陵墓 = |史跡 = |有形文化財 = |埋蔵文化財包蔵地番号 = |特記事項 = |地図 = Japan Gunma |地図2 = |アイコン = |ラベル = |ラベル位置 = }} '''月田古墳群'''（つきだこふんぐん）は、[[群馬県]][[前橋市]][[粕川町月田]]・[[粕川町室沢]]にある[[古墳時代]]後期の[[古墳群]]。
== 概要 == 月田古墳群は、群馬県前橋市粕川町月田・粕川町室沢に位置する35～40基の古墳からなるが、その多くが既に消滅している{{Sfn|小島|1982|loc=序}}{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=35}}。
1948年（昭和23年）から1955年（昭和30年）にかけて[[尾崎喜左雄]]ら[[群馬大学]]考古学研究室によって6基の古墳の[[発掘調査]]が行われた{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=16}}。このとき調査された古墳については6世紀末から7世紀後葉の古墳と結論づけられた{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=48}}が、1980年（昭和55年）・1981年（昭和56年）度の[[粕川村 (群馬県)|粕川村]]教育委員会による7基の古墳に関する調査ではむしろ7世紀後半の古墳が主体であると指摘されている{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=16}}。
自治体から史跡指定を受けている古墳は以下の2基。
* 鏡手塚古墳 - 群馬県指定史跡（1949年（昭和24年）2月8日指定） * 壇塚古墳 - 群馬県指定史跡（1951年（昭和26年）10月5日指定）<ref>{{Cite web |title=県指定文化財一覧{{!}}前橋市 |url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html |website=www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp |access-date=2024-04-21 |language=ja}}</ref>
==主要古墳一覧== {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small;width:60%" |+ 主要古墳の詳細 ! 古墳名<ref name=":0">群馬県古墳綜覧 本文一覧表編 群馬県教育委員会 P93 707番から714番（2017）より</ref> !綜覧古墳番号<ref>上毛古墳綜覧 群馬県史蹟名勝天然紀念物調査報告書第5輯 群馬縣（1938）</ref> ! 規模<ref name=":0" /> ! 形状<ref name=":0" /> !主体部 !所在地<ref name=":0" /> !調査 ! 備考 |- ! !粕川村11 | | | |粕川村室沢 |1980年（昭和55年）度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} | |- ! !粕川村12 | | |両袖型横穴式石室{{Sfn|小島|1982|pp=5-11}} |粕川町室沢 |1980年（昭和55年）度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} |7世紀後半{{Sfn|小島|1982|pp=5-11}} |- ! !粕川村16 | | | |粕川町月田 |1980年（昭和55年）度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} | |- ! !粕川村26 |14 m{{Sfn|小島|1982|pp=12-16}} |円墳{{Sfn|小島|1982|pp=12-16}} |両袖型横穴式石室{{Sfn|小島|1982|pp=12-16}} |粕川町月田 |1981年（昭和56年）度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} |7世紀後半{{Sfn|小島|1982|pp=12-16}} |- ! !粕川村34 | | | |粕川町月田 |1981年（昭和56年）度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} | |- ! 月田薬師塚古墳 !粕川村35{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=43-44}} | 不明{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=43-44}} | 円墳{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=43-44}} |横穴式石室{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=43-44}} |粕川町月田 188 |有{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=43-44}} | |- ! 壇塚古墳 !粕川村36 | 25 [[メートル|m]]{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=35-39}} | 円墳{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=35-39}} |横穴式石室{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=35-39}} |粕川町月田 207 |1950年（昭和25年）{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=35-39}} | 県史跡<ref>群馬県史跡 昭和26年10月5日指定 第5号</ref> |- ! 鏡手塚古墳 !粕川村44{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=39-42}} | 28 m{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=39-42}} | 前方後円墳{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=39-42}} |片袖型横穴式石室{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=39-42}} |粕川町月田 213外 |1948年（昭和23年）{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=39-42}} | 県史跡<ref>群馬県史跡 昭和24年2月8日指定 第2号</ref> |- ! 西塚古墳 !粕川村45{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=42-43}} | 20 m{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=42-43}} | 前方後円墳{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=42-43}} |両袖型横穴式石室{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=42-43}} |粕川町月田 213 |1948年（昭和23年）{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=42-43}} | |- ! 月田丸塚古墳（丸山古墳） !粕川村42 | 不明 | 円墳 | |粕川町月田 218-4 | | 消滅 |- ! 長峯古墳 !粕川村43{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=44-46}} | 11 m{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=44-46}} | 円墳 |片袖型横穴式石室{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=44-46}} |粕川町月田 257-1外 |1950年（昭和25年）{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|pp=44-46}} | 消滅 |- !丸塚古墳 !粕川村46{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=46}} | |円墳{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=46}} |横穴式石室{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=46}} |粕川町月田 |1950年（昭和25年）{{Sfn|粕川村誌編纂委員会|1972|p=46}} | |- ! 地蔵塚古墳 !粕川村48 | 不明 | 帆立貝型古墳 | |粕川町月田 1919-1外 | | |- ! 月田二子塚古墳 !粕川村49 | 不明 | 前方後円墳 | |粕川町月田 1918-1外 | | 消滅 |- ! !（記載漏1号） | | | | |1980年（昭和55年）度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} | |- ! !（記載漏2号） | | | | |1981年（昭和56年）度{{Sfn|小島|1982|p=2}} | |}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:月田古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Maebashi|qid=Q201613}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q85880981]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukida Kofun Cluster''' (月田古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the late [[Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan, specifically in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|qq=|1=Kasukawa Town Tsukida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|qq=|1=Kasukawa Town Murozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}}.
== Overview == The Tsukida Kofun Cluster comprises 35–40 Kofuns located in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|qq=|1=Kasukawa Town Tsukida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|qq=|1=Kasukawa Town Murozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}} in Maebashi City, but most of these have already been destroyed.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|loc=序}}{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=35}} Between 1948 and 1955, six mounds were excavated by {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|qq=|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} and the Gunma University Archaeology Department.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}} These mounds were dated from the late 6th century to the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=48}} Later investigations in 1980–1981 by the Kasukawa Village Education Board indicated a predominance of mounds constructed in the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}}
Two kofun in the cluster have been designated as prefectural historic sites: * '''Kagamitezuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated February 8, 1949) * '''Danzuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated October 5, 1951){{Cite web|title=List of Prefectural Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html|website=City of Maebashi|access-date=2024-04-21|language=ja}}
== List of Main Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small;width:60%" |+ Details of Major Kofun ! Kofun Name{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|群馬県古墳綜覧}} ! Catalog Number{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|上毛古墳綜覧}} ! Size ! Shape ! Burial Structure ! Location ! Excavation ! Notes |- ! Kagamitezuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 44 | 28 m | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | Single-shoulder horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 213 | 1948 | Designated prefectural historic site (February 8, 1949) |- ! Danzuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 36 | 25 m | Round mound | Horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 207 | 1950 | Designated prefectural historic site (October 5, 1951) |} (Additional rows omitted for brevity)
== Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|近戸神社 (前橋市粕川町月田)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|qq=|1=Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131275526}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|ja|山上多重塔|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|qq=|1=Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122341137}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|title=Kasukawa Village History|origdate=1972-12-10|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/101858|doi=10.24484/sitereports.101858}} * {{Cite book|author=Junichi Kojima|title=Tsukida Kofun Cluster B1|origdate=1982-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15291|doi=10.24484/sitereports.15291}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:月田古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q85880981]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukida Kofun Cluster''' (月田古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the late [[Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan, specifically in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|1=Kasukawa Town Tsukida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|1=Kasukawa Town Murozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}}.
== Overview == The Tsukida Kofun Cluster comprises 35–40 Kofuns located in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|1=Kasukawa Town Tsukida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|1=Kasukawa Town Murozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}} in Maebashi City, but most of these have already been destroyed.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|loc=序}}{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=35}} Between 1948 and 1955, six mounds were excavated by {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} and the Gunma University Archaeology Department.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}} These mounds were dated from the late 6th century to the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=48}} Later investigations in 1980–1981 by the Kasukawa Village Education Board indicated a predominance of mounds constructed in the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}}
Two kofun in the cluster have been designated as prefectural historic sites: * '''Kagamitezuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated February 8, 1949) * '''Danzuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated October 5, 1951){{Cite web|title=List of Prefectural Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html|website=City of Maebashi|access-date=2024-04-21|language=ja}}
== List of Main Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small;width:60%" |+ Details of Major Kofun ! Kofun Name{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|群馬県古墳綜覧}} ! Catalog Number{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|上毛古墳綜覧}} ! Size ! Shape ! Burial Structure ! Location ! Excavation ! Notes |- ! Kagamitezuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 44 | 28 m | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | Single-shoulder horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 213 | 1948 | Designated prefectural historic site (February 8, 1949) |- ! Danzuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 36 | 25 m | Round mound | Horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 207 | 1950 | Designated prefectural historic site (October 5, 1951) |} (Additional rows omitted for brevity)
== Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|近戸神社 (前橋市粕川町月田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|1=Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131275526}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|ja|山上多重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|1=Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122341137}}

== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|title=Kasukawa Village History|origdate=1972-12-10|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/101858|doi=10.24484/sitereports.101858}} * {{Cite book|author=Junichi Kojima|title=Tsukida Kofun Cluster B1|origdate=1982-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15291|doi=10.24484/sitereports.15291}}

== Overview == The Tsukida Kofun Cluster comprises 35–40 Kofuns located in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|1=Kasukawa Town Tsukida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|1=Kasukawa Town Murozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}} in Maebashi City, but most of these have already been destroyed.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|loc=序}}{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=35}} Between 1948 and 1955, six mounds were excavated by {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} and the Gunma University Archaeology Department.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}} These mounds were dated from the late 6th century to the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=48}} Later investigations in 1980–1981 by the Kasukawa Village Education Board indicated a predominance of mounds constructed in the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}}
Two kofun in the cluster have been designated as prefectural historic sites: * '''Kagamitezuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated February 8, 1949) * '''Danzuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated October 5, 1951){{Cite web|title=List of Prefectural Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html|website=City of Maebashi|access-date=2024-04-21|language=ja}}
== List of Main Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small;width:60%" |+ Details of Major Kofun ! Kofun Name{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|群馬県古墳綜覧}} ! Catalog Number{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|上毛古墳綜覧}} ! Size ! Shape ! Burial Structure ! Location ! Excavation ! Notes |- ! Kagamitezuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 44 | 28 m | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | Single-shoulder horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 213 | 1948 | Designated prefectural historic site (February 8, 1949) |- ! Danzuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 36 | 25 m | Round mound | Horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 207 | 1950 | Designated prefectural historic site (October 5, 1951) |} (Additional rows omitted for brevity)
== Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|近戸神社 (前橋市粕川町月田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|1=Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131275526}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|ja|山上多重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|1=Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122341137}}

Two kofun in the cluster have been designated as prefectural historic sites: * '''Kagamitezuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated February 8, 1949) * '''Danzuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated October 5, 1951){{Cite web|title=List of Prefectural Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html|website=City of Maebashi|access-date=2024-04-21|language=ja}}
== List of Main Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small;width:60%" |+ Details of Major Kofun ! Kofun Name{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|群馬県古墳綜覧}} ! Catalog Number{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|上毛古墳綜覧}} ! Size ! Shape ! Burial Structure ! Location ! Excavation ! Notes |- ! Kagamitezuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 44 | 28 m | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | Single-shoulder horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 213 | 1948 | Designated prefectural historic site (February 8, 1949) |- ! Danzuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 36 | 25 m | Round mound | Horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 207 | 1950 | Designated prefectural historic site (October 5, 1951) |} (Additional rows omitted for brevity)
== Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|近戸神社 (前橋市粕川町月田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|1=Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131275526}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|ja|山上多重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|1=Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122341137}}

== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|editor=Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|title=Kasukawa Village History|origdate=1972-12-10|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/101858|doi=10.24484/sitereports.101858}} * {{Cite book|author=Junichi Kojima|title=Tsukida Kofun Cluster B1|origdate=1982-03|url=https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/15291|doi=10.24484/sitereports.15291}}

Kasukawa 44 | 28 m | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | Single-shoulder horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 213 | 1948 | Designated prefectural historic site (February 8, 1949) |- ! Danzuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 36 | 25 m | Round mound | Horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 207 | 1950 | Designated prefectural historic site (October 5, 1951) |} (Additional rows omitted for brevity)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:月田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:月田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:月田古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q85880981]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukida Kofun Cluster''' (月田古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the late [[Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan, specifically in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|1=Kasukawa Town Tsukida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|1=Kasukawa Town Murozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}}.
== Overview == The Tsukida Kofun Cluster comprises 35–40 Kofuns located in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|1=Kasukawa Town Tsukida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|1=Kasukawa Town Murozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}} in Maebashi City, but most of these have already been destroyed.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|loc=序}}{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=35}} Between 1948 and 1955, six mounds were excavated by {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} and the Gunma University Archaeology Department.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}} These mounds were dated from the late 6th century to the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=48}} Later investigations in 1980–1981 by the Kasukawa Village Education Board indicated a predominance of mounds constructed in the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}}
Two kofun in the cluster have been designated as prefectural historic sites: * '''Kagamitezuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated February 8, 1949) * '''Danzuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated October 5, 1951){{Cite web|title=List of Prefectural Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html|website=City of Maebashi|access-date=2024-04-21|language=ja}}
== List of Main Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small;width:60%" |+ Details of Major Kofun ! Kofun Name{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|群馬県古墳綜覧}} ! Catalog Number{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|上毛古墳綜覧}} ! Size ! Shape ! Burial Structure ! Location ! Excavation ! Notes |- ! Kagamitezuka Kofun ! Danzuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 36 | 25 m | Round mound | Horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 207 | 1950 | Designated prefectural historic site (October 5, 1951) |} (Additional rows omitted for brevity)
== Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|近戸神社 (前橋市粕川町月田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|1=Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131275526}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|ja|山上多重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|1=Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122341137}}

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:月田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:月田古墳群]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:月田古墳群]] {{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q85880981]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Tsukida Kofun Cluster''' (月田古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the late [[Kofun period]] located in [[:en:Maebashi|Maebashi]], [[:en:Gunma Prefecture|Gunma Prefecture]], Japan, specifically in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|1=Kasukawa Town Tsukida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|1=Kasukawa Town Murozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}}.
== Overview == The Tsukida Kofun Cluster comprises 35–40 Kofuns located in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|1=Kasukawa Town Tsukida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|1=Kasukawa Town Murozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}} in Maebashi City, but most of these have already been destroyed.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|loc=序}}{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=35}} Between 1948 and 1955, six mounds were excavated by {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} and the Gunma University Archaeology Department.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}} These mounds were dated from the late 6th century to the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=48}} Later investigations in 1980–1981 by the Kasukawa Village Education Board indicated a predominance of mounds constructed in the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}}
Two kofun in the cluster have been designated as prefectural historic sites: * '''Kagamitezuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated February 8, 1949) * '''Danzuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated October 5, 1951){{Cite web|title=List of Prefectural Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html|website=City of Maebashi|access-date=2024-04-21|language=ja}}
== List of Main Kofun == {| class="wikitable" style="font-size:small;width:60%" |+ Details of Major Kofun ! Kofun Name{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|群馬県古墳綜覧}} ! Catalog Number{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|上毛古墳綜覧}} ! Size ! Shape ! Burial Structure ! Location ! Excavation ! Notes |- ! Kagamitezuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 44 | 28 m | [[:en:Zenpokoenfun|Zenpokoenfun]] | Single-shoulder horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 213 | 1948 | Designated prefectural historic site (February 8, 1949) |- ! Danzuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 36 | 25 m | Round mound | Horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 207 | 1950 | Designated prefectural historic site (October 5, 1951) |} (Additional rows omitted for brevity)
== Surrounding Area == * {{ill|Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|近戸神社 (前橋市粕川町月田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|1=Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131275526}} * {{ill|Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|ja|山上多重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|1=Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122341137}}

Catalog Number{{Sfn|Gunma Kofun Catalog|ja|上毛古墳綜覧}} ! Size ! Shape ! Burial Structure ! Location ! Excavation ! Notes |- ! Kagamitezuka Kofun ! Danzuka Kofun ! Kasukawa 36 | 25 m | Round mound | Horizontal stone chamber | Kasukawa Town Tsukida 207 | 1950 | Designated prefectural historic site (October 5, 1951) |} (Additional rows omitted for brevity)

== Overview == The Tsukida Kofun Cluster comprises 35–40 Kofuns located in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|1=Kasukawa Town Tsukida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|1=Kasukawa Town Murozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}} in Maebashi City, but most of these have already been destroyed.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|loc=序}}{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=35}} Between 1948 and 1955, six mounds were excavated by {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} and the Gunma University Archaeology Department.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}} These mounds were dated from the late 6th century to the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=48}} Later investigations in 1980–1981 by the Kasukawa Village Education Board indicated a predominance of mounds constructed in the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}}
Two kofun in the cluster have been designated as prefectural historic sites: * '''Kagamitezuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated February 8, 1949) * '''Danzuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated October 5, 1951){{Cite web|title=List of Prefectural Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html|website=City of Maebashi|access-date=2024-04-21|language=ja}}

{{Infobox ancient site|location={{ill|Maebashi|WD=Q201613}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q85880981]]
Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:07, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}}
'''Tsukida Kofun Cluster''' (月田古墳群) is a group of [[kofun]] (ancient Kofuns) from the late [[Kofun period]] located in {{ill|Maebashi|en|Maebashi|WD=Q201613|lt=Maebashi}}, {{ill|Gunma Prefecture|en|Gunma Prefecture|WD=Q129499|lt=Gunma Prefecture}}, Japan, specifically in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|1=Kasukawa Town Tsukida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|1=Kasukawa Town Murozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}}.

== Overview ==
The Tsukida Kofun Cluster comprises 35–40 Kofuns located in {{ill|Kasukawa Town Tsukida|ja|粕川町月田|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Tsukida|1=Kasukawa Town Tsukida|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123330}} and {{ill|Kasukawa Town Murozawa|ja|粕川町室沢|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kasukawa Town Murozawa|1=Kasukawa Town Murozawa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q22123417}} in Maebashi City, but most of these have already been destroyed.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|loc=序}}{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=35}} Between 1948 and 1955, six mounds were excavated by {{ill|Kisao Ozaki|ja|尾崎喜左雄|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kisao Ozaki|1=Kisao Ozaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q21698487}} and the Gunma University Archaeology Department.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}} These mounds were dated from the late 6th century to the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kasukawa Village History Compilation Committee|1972|p=48}} Later investigations in 1980–1981 by the Kasukawa Village Education Board indicated a predominance of mounds constructed in the late 7th century.{{Sfn|Kojima|1982|p=16}}

Two kofun in the cluster have been designated as prefectural historic sites:
* '''Kagamitezuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated February 8, 1949)
* '''Danzuka Kofun''' - Gunma Prefecture Historic Site (designated October 5, 1951){{Cite web|title=List of Prefectural Cultural Properties|url=https://www.city.maebashi.gunma.jp/soshiki/kyoiku/bunkazaihogo/gyomu/3/2/5107.html|website=City of Maebashi|access-date=2024-04-21|language=ja}}

== Surrounding Area ==
* {{ill|Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|ja|近戸神社 (前橋市粕川町月田)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|1=Chikado Shrine (Maebashi)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131275526}}
* {{ill|Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|ja|山上多重塔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|1=Yamashiro Multi-story Tower|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q122341137}}
